diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanism strongly predisposes to corneal surface injury or retained foreign body.
Indicates a significant defect affecting the visual axis, mandating urgent formal assessment.
Ciliary spasm from corneal nerve irritation classically produces marked photophobia and blepharospasm.

symptoms.symptom_name: No visible foreign body and pupil reported round/reactive by bystander

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Somewhat reassuring but a non-clinician bystander assessment cannot reliably exclude a penetrating injury; formal slit-lamp exam is still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked conjunctival injection and ciliary flush on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ciliary flush reflects anterior segment inflammation; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive fluorescein stain showing a large corneal epithelial defect

investigation_details.investigation_name: Fluorescein staining under cobalt blue light (slit lamp)
investigation_details.expected_result: Large corneal epithelial defect confirming abrasion
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis and defect size unconfirmed; telehealth cannot perform this and should recommend it occur as part of urgent ED/ophthalmic assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Definitive diagnostic finding for corneal epithelial defect.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: High-velocity particulate trauma with reduced acuity and marked photophobia mandates urgent same-day ophthalmic/ED assessment to confirm the extent of injury and exclude a penetrating component.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms corneal abrasion, defines defect size, and screens for retained foreign body or deeper injury; cannot be performed via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Eyelid eversion to check for subtarsal foreign body
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Retained material under the upper lid is a common cause of persistent symptoms and recurrent corneal trauma if missed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT orbit as routine first step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not first-line for a presentation without suspicion of a large or radio-opaque intraocular/orbital foreign body; slit-lamp exam comes first.
